questions.question: What is the primary function of a database?
options: A) To store and manage data
B) To perform complex queries
C) To provide user interface
D) To manage database security
questions.explanation: A database is a collection of organized data that is stored in a way that allows for efficient retrieval and manipulation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a relational database?
options: A) Ability to store only structured data
B) Ability to store both structured and unstructured data
C) Ability to store multiple types of data
D) Ability to store data in a single file
questions.explanation: Relational databases are able to store multiple types of data, including structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a primary key in a database?
options: A) To uniquely identify each record
B) To organize data in a specific order
C) To filter data based on specific criteria
D) To perform data backups
questions.explanation: A primary key is used to uniquely identify each record in a database, allowing for efficient retrieval and manipulation of data.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using a database management system?
options: A) Improved data security
B) Increased data storage capacity
C) Improved data retrieval and manipulation capabilities
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: One of the advantages of using a database management system is improved data retrieval and manipulation capabilities, as well as improved data security and increased data storage capacity.

questions.question: What is the purpose of normalization in database design?
options: A) To improve data security
B) To reduce data redundancy
C) To increase data storage capacity
D) To improve data retrieval and manipulation capabilities
questions.explanation: Normalization in database design is used to reduce data redundancy, improve data consistency, and improve data retrieval and manipulation capabilities.
